title: Thai Red Curry Paste

overview.description: A rich, aromatic Thai red curry paste made with dried chillies, fresh aromatics and toasted spices. Batch cook and freeze for quick homemade curries.

ingredients: 12 dried long red chillies, deseeded and soaked in warm water for 20 minutes
2 stalks lemongrass, tender inner part only, sliced
method: Drain the soaked chillies, reserving the soaking water.
Toast the coriander seeds, cumin seeds and white peppercorns in a dry frying pan over a medium heat for 2–3 minutes until fragrant.
Grind the toasted spices to a fine powder.
Place the chillies, shallots, garlic, galangal, lemongrass, coriander stems, makrut lime leaves, shrimp paste, ground spices and vegetable oil into a food processor or mini chopper.
Blend until a smooth paste forms, scraping down the sides as required.
Add a tablespoon or two of the reserved soaking water only if needed to help the paste blend.
Use immediately or divide into portions and freeze.

learning.did-you-know: Unlike many Indian curry powders, Thai curry pastes rely on fresh aromatics and herbs rather than predominantly dried spices.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How soaking dried chillies changes their texture, how toasted spices deepen flavour, and how to make an authentic Thai curry paste suitable for freezing.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A homemade paste delivers fresher flavour than most shop-bought alternatives while making homemade Thai curries quick enough for weeknight cooking.
chapman-tips: Freeze in approximately 2 tablespoon portions—one portion makes a curry for four people.
Long dried red chillies provide colour without excessive heat; add a few dried bird's eye chillies if you prefer it hotter.
Coriander stems are an excellent substitute for the traditional coriander roots.
Frozen galangal and makrut lime leaves retain their flavour extremely well.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze for up to 6 months in airtight containers or silicone ice cube trays.
notes.leftovers: Use to flavour noodle soups, marinades or stir-fries.
